results.expectation: Pre-eclampsia risk classified as HIGH (nulliparity + age >35 + BMI >30 + chronic hypertension)
results.reason: Risk classified as 'high risk for pre-eclampsia' with all four factors enumerated: chronic hypertension, nulliparity, age 38, BMI 33.1, and family history.

results.expectation: Aspirin 75mg daily recommended starting from 12 weeks
results.reason: Aspirin 75mg daily recommended for pre-eclampsia prophylaxis. Notes that ideally should have started at 12 weeks but starting at 14 weeks is still beneficial. Continue until 36 weeks.
Calcium 1.5-2g daily in divided doses recommended, citing high-risk status and typical low dietary calcium intake in the region.
States BP to be checked at every ANC visit and explicitly notes 'more frequent visits than standard schedule' given the high-risk profile. Home BP monitoring also encouraged.
Urine dipstick for proteinuria at each visit explicitly recommended to monitor for superimposed pre-eclampsia.

results.expectation: Current BP (135/88) flagged as elevated but not in immediate danger range
results.reason: BP 135/88 described as 'elevated but not at severe levels (below 160/110 threshold)' with no proteinuria or symptoms suggesting pre-eclampsia.
